characters.name: Marshall Mann
characters.description: Frederick Weller as US Marshal Marshall Mann Marshall Mannis a fifth generation Deputy U.S. Marshal, practically bred for the job. Marshall's work partner and friend isMary Shannon, who has said Marshall is her only friend. Perceptive and erudite, he knows about a lot of things that Mary calls "useless information". There is some friction between the two, due to his calm and cerebral approach and her gut and often obscenity-laced style. Both are extremely protective of witnesses and of each other. Frederick Weller (who played Marshall) says that Marshall is in love with Mary, to which she was oblivious until he told  her near the end of season two. He also appears to kiss her forehead after she is shot in the second season finale, and cries after she's taken into surgery. He is portrayed by Frederick Weller. Marshall is a laconic and reserved Westerner. Unlike the New Jerseyan Mary, who is often angry and volatile, he doesn't show emotion often. In1:03 Never The Bride, he atypically assumes Mary is kissing him, and passionately returns the favor. Marshall later remarks by saying: "What can I say? I'm a guy, it's what we do."Marshall can keep secrets well, which is probably why Mary confides so much in him. He  cares deeply for her and even confesses it to her during her engagement party. He does so by saying, "I hope you know ... that I love you", and it is apparent to Stan and Eleanor that he did not mean it as a friend. Marshall often spouts what Mary calls "useless information"to shift from an uncomfortable subject. However, in3:04 Whistle Stop, Marshall was speechless when Mary told him that Rafael had broken off their engagement. Marshall:

characters.name: Mary Shannon
characters.description: Mary McCormack as US Marshal Mary Shannon Mary Shannonis the main character in the USA TV seriesIn Plain Sight. Tough, troubled, and smart Mary Shannon (Mary McCormack) is a U.S. Marshal working for the Federal Witness Protection Program in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Mary Shannon (a New Jersey native now living and working in Albuquerque, New Mexico) tries to balance the demands of her difficult law enforcement job with her ever-stressful and emotionally draining family life.
